entries.title: Change Your Email Address
entries.summary: How a customer changes the email address on their Nimbus account and confirms the change.
entries.tags: change email, update email, account email, verify
entries.text:
---
summary: "how a customer changes the email address on their nimbus account and confirms the change."
category: "account"
tags: "change email, update email, account email, verify"
url: "https://help.nimbus.example/account/change-email"
---

# change your email address

1. open **settings → account → email**.
2. enter the new email address and your current password.
3. we send a confirmation link to the **new** address — it's valid for **24 hours**.
4. the change takes effect once the customer clicks the link.

a notification is also sent to the **old** address. if the customer didn't make
the change, they should reset the password immediately and contact support.

## notes

- sign-in uses the new email straight after confirmation.
- shared links and collaborators are unaffected.
- the email on file for **billing receipts** updates automatically too.

entries.title: Enable Two Factor Authentication
entries.summary: How to turn on two-factor authentication (2FA) with an authenticator app, plus recovery codes.
entries.tags: 2fa, two factor, mfa, authenticator, security code, recovery codes
entries.text:
---
summary: "how to turn on two-factor authentication (2fa) with an authenticator app, plus recovery codes."
category: "account"
tags: "2fa, two factor, mfa, authenticator, security code, recovery codes"
url: "https://help.nimbus.example/account/two-factor"
---

# enable two-factor authentication

two-factor authentication (2fa) adds a one-time code on top of the password.

## set it up

1. open **settings → security → two-factor authentication**.
2. click **enable** and scan the qr code with an authenticator app
   (google authenticator, authy, 1password, etc.).
3. enter the 6-digit code to confirm.
4. **save the recovery codes** shown on screen — there are **10** of them, each
   usable once.

## lost access to the authenticator

- use one of the **recovery codes** at sign-in.
- no recovery codes? the account owner must verify identity by email before
  support can disable 2fa. this can take up to **2 business days**.

> recovery codes are the only offline way back in. tell customers to store them
> somewhere safe, not inside nimbus itself.

entries.title: Reset Your Password
entries.summary: Steps for resetting a forgotten Nimbus password and what to do if the reset email doesn't arrive.
entries.tags: password, reset, forgot password, login, cannot sign in
entries.text:
---
summary: "steps for resetting a forgotten nimbus password and what to do if the reset email doesn't arrive."
category: "account"
tags: "password, reset, forgot password, login, cannot sign in"
url: "https://help.nimbus.example/account/reset-password"
---

# reset your password

## if you're signed out

1. on the sign-in page, click **forgot password?**
2. enter your account email and submit.
3. open the reset email and click **reset password** (the link is valid for **1 hour**).
4. choose a new password — minimum **10 characters**, with at least one number.

## if you're signed in

open **settings → security → change password**. you'll need your current password.

## reset email didn't arrive

- check the spam/junk folder.
- confirm the email matches the one on the account (try alternate addresses).
- wait **5 minutes** — delivery can be delayed.
- still nothing? the address may not have a nimbus account. have the customer
  confirm their signup email.

> resetting a password does **not** sign out other devices. to do that, use
> **settings → security → sign out all sessions**.

entries.title: Cancel Your Subscription
entries.summary: How to cancel a paid Nimbus plan, when it takes effect, and what happens to stored files.
entries.tags: cancel, cancellation, unsubscribe, downgrade, end subscription
entries.text:
---
summary: "how to cancel a paid nimbus plan, when it takes effect, and what happens to stored files."
category: "billing"
tags: "cancel, cancellation, unsubscribe, downgrade, end subscription"
url: "https://help.nimbus.example/billing/cancel"
---

# cancel your subscription

## how to cancel

1. open **settings → billing → plan**.
2. click **cancel subscription** and confirm.
3. the plan stays active until the **end of the current billing period** — no
   further charges after that.

## what happens to files

- at the end of the period the account moves to the **free** plan (5 gb).
- if stored data exceeds **5 gb**, files become **read-only**: the customer can
  view, download and delete, but not add new files until under the limit.
- files are **never automatically deleted** on cancellation.

## reactivating

re-subscribe any time from **settings → billing**. read-only files become
editable again as soon as the paid plan is active.

> want money back for the latest charge too? check [request a refund](request-a-refund.md)
> for the 14-day eligibility window.

entries.title: Plans And Pricing
entries.summary: Full breakdown of Nimbus plans, storage limits, prices and what each tier includes.
entries.tags: plans, pricing, price, tiers, storage, free, plus, family, business
entries.text:
---
summary: "full breakdown of nimbus plans, storage limits, prices and what each tier includes."
category: "billing"
tags: "plans, pricing, price, tiers, storage, free, plus, family, business"
url: "https://help.nimbus.example/billing/plans"
---

# plans and pricing

| plan | storage | price (monthly) | price (yearly) | users |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| free | **5 gb** | $0 | $0 | 1 |
| plus | **2 tb** | **$9.99** | **$99/yr** (2 months free) | 1 |
| family | **6 tb** | **$19.99** | **$199/yr** | up to 5 |
| business | **5 tb/user** | **$15/user** | **$150/user/yr** | 3+ |

## what's included

- **free** — basic sync and sharing, 30-day file history.
- **plus** — everything in free, **180-day** file history, larger upload limits.
- **family** — plus features for up to 5 people, each with a private space.
- **business** — admin console, audit log, and **365-day** file history.

## changing plans

- **upgrades** take effect immediately; the new price is **prorated** for the
  current period.
- **downgrades** take effect at the **next renewal date**. the account must be
  under the new plan's storage limit first.

see also: [update payment method](update-payment-method.md) ·
[cancel your subscription](cancel-your-subscription.md)

entries.title: Request A Refund
entries.summary: The Nimbus refund policy and how to process a refund request within the eligibility window.
entries.tags: refund, money back, reimbursement, charge, cancel charge
entries.text:
---
summary: "the nimbus refund policy and how to process a refund request within the eligibility window."
category: "billing"
tags: "refund, money back, reimbursement, charge, cancel charge"
url: "https://help.nimbus.example/billing/refunds"
---

# request a refund

## eligibility

- refunds are available within **14 days** of a charge.
- applies to the **most recent** payment only.
- annual plans are eligible for a **prorated** refund within the 14-day window.

## how to process

1. confirm the charge date in **settings → billing → history**.
2. verify it falls within the **14-day** window.
3. submit the refund in the billing console with a reason code.
4. refunds return to the original payment method in **5–10 business days**.

## not eligible?

if the charge is older than 14 days, a refund generally cannot be issued. offer
to [cancel the subscription](cancel-your-subscription.md) so the customer isn't
billed again, and escalate to a supervisor if they dispute the policy.

> always confirm the customer's identity on the account before discussing or
> issuing a refund.

entries.title: Update Payment Method
entries.summary: How customers update their card or payment method, and what happens when a payment fails.
entries.tags: payment method, card, credit card, update card, failed payment, declined
entries.text:
---
summary: "how customers update their card or payment method, and what happens when a payment fails."
category: "billing"
tags: "payment method, card, credit card, update card, failed payment, declined"
url: "https://help.nimbus.example/billing/payment-method"
---

# update payment method

## update a card

1. open **settings → billing → payment method**.
2. click **update** and enter the new card details.
3. save. the new card is charged at the next renewal.

accepted: **visa, mastercard, american express**, and paypal.

## when a payment fails

we retry a failed charge on this schedule:

| attempt | when |
|---|---|
| 1st retry | **day 1** after failure |
| 2nd retry | **day 3** |
| 3rd retry | **day 7** |

- the account stays on its paid plan during retries (a **7-day grace period**).
- if all retries fail, the account is downgraded to **free** and files over the
  5 gb limit become **read-only** until storage is freed or payment succeeds.
- no files are ever deleted due to a failed payment.

entries.title: Share Files And Folders
entries.summary: How to share Nimbus files and folders via link or invitation, including permissions and expiry.
entries.tags: share, sharing, link, collaborate, permissions, invite, expiry
entries.text:
---
summary: "how to share nimbus files and folders via link or invitation, including permissions and expiry."
category: "features"
tags: "share, sharing, link, collaborate, permissions, invite, expiry"
url: "https://help.nimbus.example/features/sharing"
---

# share files and folders

## share a link

1. right-click a file or folder → **share → copy link**.
2. anyone with the link can **view and download** by default.
3. (plus and above) set a **password** or an **expiry date** on the link.

## invite collaborators

1. right-click → **share → invite people**.
2. enter their email and choose a permission level:

| permission | can view | can edit | can re-share |
|---|---|---|---|
| viewer | yes | no | no |
| editor | yes | yes | no |
| manager | yes | yes | yes |

collaborators need a nimbus account to edit; viewers do not.

## stop sharing

open the item's **share** dialog and click **remove** next to a person, or
**disable link** to revoke link access. changes apply immediately.

entries.title: Install The Desktop App
entries.summary: How to download, install and sign in to the Nimbus desktop app on Windows, macOS and Linux.
entries.tags: install, desktop app, download, setup, sync folder
entries.text:
---
summary: "how to download, install and sign in to the nimbus desktop app on windows, macos and linux."
category: "getting started"
tags: "install, desktop app, download, setup, sync folder"
url: "https://help.nimbus.example/getting-started/desktop-app"
---

# install the desktop app

the desktop app creates a **nimbus folder** on your computer. anything you put in
it syncs to the cloud and your other devices.

## install

1. go to **nimbus.example/download** and choose your operating system.
2. run the installer and follow the prompts.
3. sign in with your nimbus email and password.
4. choose where to put your nimbus folder (default: your home directory).

## supported systems

| os | minimum version |
|---|---|
| windows | 10 or later |
| macos | 12 (monterey) or later |
| linux | ubuntu 20.04+ / fedora 36+ |

## selective sync

to save disk space, open **settings → sync** and uncheck folders you don't want
stored locally. they stay in the cloud and remain accessible from the web app.

if files don't appear after install, see [files not syncing](../troubleshooting/files-not-syncing.md).

entries.title: Welcome To Nimbus
entries.summary: Overview of what Nimbus is, how accounts work, and where new users should start.
entries.tags: intro, overview, getting started, new account, signup
entries.text:
---
summary: "overview of what nimbus is, how accounts work, and where new users should start."
category: "getting started"
tags: "intro, overview, getting started, new account, signup"
url: "https://help.nimbus.example/getting-started/welcome"
---

# welcome to nimbus

nimbus is cloud file storage that syncs your files across all your devices. this
article is the starting point for new customers.

## what you get

- files stored securely in the cloud and synced to every signed-in device.
- a desktop app (windows, macos, linux), mobile apps (ios, android), and a web app.
- file sharing via links or by inviting collaborators.

## first steps

1. create an account at **nimbus.example/signup** with your email and a password.
2. verify your email — we send a confirmation link valid for **24 hours**.
3. [install the desktop app](install-the-desktop-app.md) to start syncing.
4. (recommended) [enable two-factor authentication](../account/enable-two-factor-authentication.md).

## storage by plan

| plan | storage | price |
|---|---|---|
| free | **5 gb** | $0 |
| plus | **2 tb** | $9.99/mo |
| family | **6 tb** (up to 5 people) | $19.99/mo |

see [plans and pricing](../billing/plans-and-pricing.md) for full details.

entries.title: How We Protect Your Data
entries.summary: Plain-language overview of how Nimbus encrypts and protects customer data, for answering privacy questions.
entries.tags: security, privacy, encryption, gdpr, data protection, delete account
entries.text:
---
summary: "plain-language overview of how nimbus encrypts and protects customer data, for answering privacy questions."
category: "security"
tags: "security, privacy, encryption, gdpr, data protection, delete account"
url: "https://help.nimbus.example/security/data-protection"
---

# how we protect your data

## encryption

- files are encrypted **in transit** (tls 1.2+) and **at rest** (aes-256).
- encryption keys are managed by nimbus and rotated regularly.

## access and privacy

- only the account owner and people they share with can access their files.
- nimbus staff cannot read file contents; support can see file **names and
  metadata** only when needed to resolve a ticket, and only with your consent.

## data residency

customer data is stored in the region selected at signup: **eu** or **us**.
the region cannot be changed after the account is created.

## deleting your data

- deleting a file moves it to **trash**, where it's recoverable for **30 days**
  before permanent removal.
- deleting the **account** removes all files and personal data within **30 days**.
  this is irreversible — confirm identity before processing such a request.

> for formal data-export or erasure requests (e.g. gdpr), route the customer to
> **privacy@nimbus.example** and log the request.

entries.title: Files Not Syncing
entries.summary: Troubleshooting steps when files won't sync between the desktop app and the cloud.
entries.tags: sync, not syncing, stuck, upload, pending, sync error, offline
entries.text:
---
summary: "troubleshooting steps when files won't sync between the desktop app and the cloud."
category: "troubleshooting"
tags: "sync, not syncing, stuck, upload, pending, sync error, offline"
url: "https://help.nimbus.example/troubleshooting/sync"
---

# files not syncing

work through these in order; most sync problems are fixed by step 3.

1. **check the connection.** the app must show **connected** (green) in the tray
   icon. if it shows offline, confirm internet access.
2. **check storage.** a full account pauses uploads. free space or upgrade — see
   [plans and pricing](../billing/plans-and-pricing.md).
3. **pause and resume sync.** tray icon → **pause syncing**, wait 10 seconds,
   then **resume**. this clears most stuck transfers.
4. **check the file name.** these characters block syncing on some systems:
   `< > : " / \ | ? *`. rename the file to remove them.
5. **restart the app.** fully quit nimbus and reopen it.
6. **re-link the account.** settings → account → **unlink this device**, then
   sign in again. no files are lost — they re-download from the cloud.

## still stuck?

collect the sync log from **settings → advanced → export logs** and escalate to
second-line support with the file name and account email.

## common error codes

| code | meaning | fix |
|---|---|---|
| **e-101** | no connection | check network, then resume sync |
| **e-204** | storage full | free space or upgrade plan |
| **e-307** | invalid file name | rename to remove blocked characters |
| **e-500** | server error | wait 15 minutes and retry; escalate if it persists |
